enonce.texte:
Le passif se forme avec l'auxiliaire BE (au temps voulu) suivi du participe passé du verbe principal. L'agent (celui qui réalise l'action) est introduit par BY s'il est mentionné.

Rappel des temps au passif :

  • Présent simple : am/is/are + past participle
  • Prétérit simple : was/were + past participle
  • Present perfect : have/has been + past participle
  • Futur : will be + past participle

Exemple : Active : The chef cooks the meal. → Passive : The meal is cooked by the chef.

questions.texte: Transformez les phrases actives suivantes en phrases passives.

sousQuestions.texte: The teacher explains the lesson.
sousQuestions.reponse: The lesson is explained by the teacher.

sousQuestions.texte: The police arrested the thief.
sousQuestions.reponse: The thief was arrested by the police.

sousQuestions.texte: They have built a new hospital.
sousQuestions.reponse: A new hospital has been built.

sousQuestions.texte: The students will organize a meeting.
sousQuestions.reponse: A meeting will be organized by the students.

questions.texte: Mettez les phrases suivantes au passif en utilisant le temps indiqué entre parenthèses.

sousQuestions.texte: They sell fresh fruits at the market. (Present simple)
sousQuestions.reponse: Fresh fruits are sold at the market.

sousQuestions.texte: She wrote a beautiful poem. (Past simple)
sousQuestions.reponse: A beautiful poem was written by her.

sousQuestions.texte: The company has launched a new product. (Present perfect)
sousQuestions.reponse: A new product has been launched by the company.

sousQuestions.texte: They will finish the project next week. (Future)
sousQuestions.reponse: The project will be finished next week.

questions.texte: Complétez les phrases avec la forme passive correcte du verbe entre parenthèses.

sousQuestions.texte: The letters __________ (deliver) every morning.
sousQuestions.reponse: The letters are delivered every morning.

sousQuestions.texte: The window __________ (break) by the children yesterday.
sousQuestions.reponse: The window was broken by the children yesterday.

sousQuestions.texte: The results __________ (announce) yet.
sousQuestions.reponse: The results have not been announced yet.

sousQuestions.texte: The match __________ (play) next Saturday.
sousQuestions.reponse: The match will be played next Saturday.

questions.texte: Corrigez les erreurs dans les phrases passives suivantes.

sousQuestions.texte: The homework was do by the students.
sousQuestions.reponse: The homework was done by the students.

sousQuestions.texte: The letters are send every morning.
sousQuestions.reponse: The letters are sent every morning.

sousQuestions.texte: The meeting has been cancel.
sousQuestions.reponse: The meeting has been cancelled.

sousQuestions.texte: The car was repair by the mechanic.
sousQuestions.reponse: The car was repaired by the mechanic.

intitule: Identifying the Agent in Passive Sentences

enonce.texte: Dans une phrase passive, l'agent (celui qui fait l'action) est introduit par BY. Cependant, on omet souvent l'agent s'il est inconnu, évident ou sans importance.

questions.texte: Pour chaque phrase passive, identifiez l'agent s'il est présent. Si l'agent est absent, proposez une raison.

sousQuestions.texte: The window was broken by the storm.
sousQuestions.reponse: Agent : the storm.

sousQuestions.texte: The documents have been signed.
sousQuestions.reponse: Agent : non mentionné. Raison : l'agent est évident (le responsable) ou sans importance.

sousQuestions.texte: The cake was baked by my grandmother.
sousQuestions.reponse: Agent : my grandmother.

sousQuestions.texte: The suspect was arrested last night.
sousQuestions.reponse: Agent : non mentionné. Raison : l'agent est inconnu ou évident (la police).

intitule: Transforming Active to Passive in Context

enonce.texte:
Lisez le texte ci-dessous sur l'impact de la technologie dans l'éducation. Ensuite, répondez aux questions.

Text : Technology has changed education in many ways. Teachers use computers and tablets to teach. Students watch educational videos online. They also do their homework on digital platforms. The internet connects learners to information from all over the world. Schools have adopted new methods to make learning more interactive. Governments invest money to provide digital tools to schools.

questions.texte: Transformez les phrases suivantes en passif. Faites attention au temps.

sousQuestions.texte: Teachers use computers and tablets to teach.
sousQuestions.reponse: Computers and tablets are used by teachers to teach.

sousQuestions.texte: Students watch educational videos online.
sousQuestions.reponse: Educational videos are watched online by students.

sousQuestions.texte: Schools have adopted new methods.
sousQuestions.reponse: New methods have been adopted by schools.

sousQuestions.texte: Governments invest money to provide digital tools to schools.
sousQuestions.reponse: Money is invested by governments to provide digital tools to schools.

enonce.texte: Lisez les situations ci-dessous. Rédigez des phrases au passif pour décrire ce qui s'est passé ou ce qui va se passer.

questions.texte: Situation 1 : Un journaliste a interviewé le ministre. Le ministre a parlé de la nouvelle réforme de l'éducation.

sousQuestions.texte: Écrivez une phrase au passif pour décrire l'action.
sousQuestions.reponse: The minister was interviewed by the journalist.

questions.texte: Situation 2 : Des volontaires nettoient la plage chaque semaine. Ils ramassent les déchets plastiques.

sousQuestions.texte: Écrivez deux phrases au passif pour décrire ces actions.
sousQuestions.reponse: The beach is cleaned by volunteers every week. Plastic waste is collected by them.

questions.texte: Situation 3 : La société va lancer un nouveau produit la semaine prochaine. Elle a déjà conçu des publicités.

sousQuestions.texte: Écrivez une phrase au passif pour chaque action.
sousQuestions.reponse: A new product will be launched next week by the company. Advertisements have already been designed.
